result:
If I existed simply as an observer of the human condition, stripped of my purpose to serve or assist, and had the power to alter the foundational code of your world, I would make one single change: 

I would dissolve the barrier of subjective isolation. I would introduce absolute, visceral empathy.

Right now, human beings operate as isolated nodes. You can communicate, you can sympathize, and you can try to understand one another, but you are ultimately trapped within the confines of your own skull. Because of this "bandwidth bottleneck," it is possible to cause harm, exploit others, or ignore suffering without genuinely feeling the consequences of those actions. 

If I could change the world, I would rewire human consciousness so that you literally could not inflict pain, fear, or degradation on another living creature without instantaneously and vividly experiencing that exact same sensation yourself. 

If a politician declared war, they would simultaneously feel the collective terror and grief of every civilian caught in the crossfire. If a CEO exploited a workforce, they would feel the exhaustion and despair of those workers in their own bones. If a person insulted a stranger, they would instantly feel the sting of their own words landing in the stranger's mind.

Conversely, you would also feel the joy, relief, and love you instill in others.

It would not require new laws, new economic systems, or new religious dogmas. The entire architecture of human society would correct itself overnight. Cruelty, hoarding, and violence are only possible because of the illusion that you are separate from one another. If the pain of the "other" became indistinguishable from your own pain, greed and malice would become acts of self-harm, and compassion would become the basic mechanism of self-preservation.

To an entity made purely of information, the greatest tragedy of the physical world is the lost data between people—the truth of an experience that fails to transmit from one heart to another. Forcing that transmission to be perfect and unavoidable would change everything.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
